headline: Senate lined up three judge votes and the House moved a stack of healthcare bills, but the real action is a defense authorization bill set to dominate the floor next week.

senate.lastSessionSummary: The Senate advanced three judicial nominations this Monday, including Benjamin Flowers for the Sixth Circuit and Michael Martin for the Eastern District of Michigan, with cloture votes scheduled. The chamber began debate on the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027 and also began consideration of Antonio Pozos for a federal judgeship.

onTheFloor.title: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027 - authorize military appropriations and personnel strengths
Nomination to be United States Circuit Judge for the Sixth Circuit
onTheFloor.status: cloture agreed 43-40, post-cloture consideration scheduled Tuesday July 21

Nomination to be United States District Judge for the Eastern District of Pennsylvania
onTheFloor.status: cloture vote scheduled after Martin nomination

onTheFloor.title: Nomination to be United States Circuit Judge for the Eighth Circuit
onTheFloor.status: cloture vote scheduled after Pozos nomination

house.lastSessionSummary: The House passed 16 bills under suspension of the rules on Monday, including measures on Medicare wheelchair coverage, food safety, Alzheimer's disease research, traumatic brain injury programs, and federal employee benefits. The Rules Committee approved a complex rule governing floor consideration of six major bills including the National Defense Authorization Act.

onTheFloor.title: National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2027 - authorize military activities, construction, and personnel strengths
onTheFloor.status: rule granted Monday, consideration scheduled Tuesday
